stem: The vector field \( \mathbf{F}(x,y) = \left( ([[a]])y + ([[b]]),\; ([[a]])x + ([[c]]) \right) \) is conservative. Evaluate \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \), where \( C \) is any smooth curve from \( A = ([[x1]], [[y1]]) \) to \( B = ([[x2]], [[y2]]) \).
solution: A potential is \( \varphi(x,y) = ([[a]])xy + ([[b]])x + ([[c]])y \), since \( \nabla\varphi = \mathbf{F} \). By the fundamental theorem for line integrals, \( \int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} = \varphi(B) - \varphi(A) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes \( \varphi(A) - \varphi(B) \), reversing the endpoints.
This adds \( \varphi(B) + \varphi(A) \) instead of subtracting.
This keeps only the \( xy \)-part of the potential, dropping the linear terms.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: The vector field \( \mathbf{F}(x,y) = \left( 2([[a]])x + ([[c]])y,\; 2([[b]])y + ([[c]])x \right) \) is conservative, with potential \( \varphi(x,y) = ([[a]])x^{2} + ([[b]])y^{2} + ([[c]])xy \). Evaluate \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \), where \( C \) is any smooth curve from \( A = ([[x1]], [[y1]]) \) to \( B = ([[x2]], [[y2]]) \).
solution: The given field is \( \nabla\varphi \) for \( \varphi(x,y) = ([[a]])x^{2} + ([[b]])y^{2} + ([[c]])xy \). By the fundamental theorem for line integrals, \( \int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} = \varphi(B) - \varphi(A) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes \( \varphi(A) - \varphi(B) \), reversing the endpoints.
This adds \( \varphi(B) + \varphi(A) \) instead of subtracting.
This drops the cross term \( cxy \) of the potential at both endpoints.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.

stem: The vector field \( \mathbf{F}(x,y,z) = \left( ([[a]])y + ([[b]])z,\; ([[a]])x + ([[c]])z,\; ([[b]])x + ([[c]])y \right) \) is conservative in \( \mathbb{R}^{3} \). Evaluate \( \displaystyle\int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} \), where \( C \) is any smooth curve from \( A = ([[x1]], [[y1]], [[z1]]) \) to \( B = ([[x2]], [[y2]], [[z2]]) \).
solution: A potential is \( \varphi(x,y,z) = ([[a]])xy + ([[b]])xz + ([[c]])yz \), since \( \nabla\varphi = \mathbf{F} \). By the fundamental theorem for line integrals, \( \int_C \mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} = \varphi(B) - \varphi(A) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This computes \( \varphi(A) - \varphi(B) \), reversing the endpoints.
This adds \( \varphi(B) + \varphi(A) \) instead of subtracting.
This keeps only the \( xy \)-term of the potential, dropping the other two cross terms.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
This is off by two from the correct value.
